title: Linked Lists
analogy: A linked list is like a scavenger hunt — each clue card tells you the location of the next clue. You must follow the chain, one step at a time.

overview.summary: Linked lists are chains of discrete nodes connected by pointers.
learningObjectives: Learn how to traverse iteratively and recursively
overview.interviewRelevance: The ultimate test of your attention to detail and null-pointer safety.

paragraphs: Browser history (back/forward), music playlists (skip to next), and undo/redo stacks all use linked lists.
The reason your browser's back button is instant — it just follows a pointer to the previous page node, no copying, no reindexing.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: A linked list is just a scavenger hunt. The first clue is the 'head', and each clue points to the location of the next clue.

definition.technicalDefinition: A linked list is a linear data structure where elements are not stored at contiguous memory locations. Instead, each element points to the next.

vocabulary.definition: An object containing data and a reference to the next node.
A reference to the very first node in the list.
The last node in the list, its next pointer is null.
The memory address reference linking one node to another.

keyProperties: Dynamic size, nodes are created individually in memory.
O(1) insertion and deletion at the current pointer.
O(N) search because you must traverse from the head sequentially.

operations.name: Insert at Head
operations.description: Add a new node to the front of the list, making it the new head.

code.python:
new_node = Node(5)
new_node.next = head
head = new_node

operations.visualNote: A new box appears on the left, an arrow points to the old head.

operations.name: Delete Node
operations.description: Rewire the previous node to skip the deleted node.
operations.timeComplexity: O(1) if prev known, else O(n)
operations.visualNote: The arrow from prev is repointed to bypass the target node.

operations.name: Search / Traverse
operations.description: Follow next pointers continuously until a null or target is met.
operations.visualNote: A pointer marker slowly moves node by node along the chain.

practice.title: Reverse Linked List
practice.keyInsight: Track prev, curr, and next cautiously.

practice.title: Linked List Cycle
practice.keyInsight: Fast and slow pointers (tortoise and hare).

practice.title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
practice.keyInsight: Two pointers spaced exactly N nodes apart.

practice.title: Copy List with Random Pointer
practice.keyInsight: Hash map maps original nodes to cloned nodes.

practice.title: LRU Cache
practice.keyInsight: Doubly linked list combined with a hash map.
